ddls:
CREATE TABLE "districts" (
  "district_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each district */,
  "district_name" TEXT /* Name of the district */,
  "state" TEXT /* State where the district is located */,
  "district_area" REAL /* Area of the district */,
  "district_population_density" REAL /* Population density of the district */,
  "geographic_coordinates" TEXT /* Geographic coordinates of the district */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("district_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "crimes" (
  "crime_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each crime type */,
  "crime_name" TEXT /* Name of the crime (e.g., Stalking, Assault) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the crime */,
  "crime_category" TEXT /* Category of the crime (e.g., violent, non-violent) */,
  "crime_description_long" TEXT /* Detailed description of the crime */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("crime_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "crime_statistics" (
  "statistic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each statistic */,
  "district_id" INTEGER /* ID of the district */,
  "crime_id" INTEGER /* ID of the crime type */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the statistic was recorded */,
  "count" INTEGER /* Number of crimes committed */,
  "crime_statistic_source" TEXT /* Source of the crime statistic */,
  "crime_statistic_methodology" TEXT /* Methodology used to collect and calculate the crime statistic */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("statistic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_crime_statistics_district_id FOREIGN KEY ("district_id") REFERENCES districts ("district_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_crime_statistics_crime_id FOREIGN KEY ("crime_id") REFERENCES crimes ("crime_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., administrator, analyst, law enforcement) */,
  "user_password" TEXT /* Password of the user (hashed for security) */,
  "user_last_login" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the statistic */,
  "district_id" INTEGER /* ID of the district */,
  "crime_id" INTEGER /* ID of the crime type */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the statistic was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "access_ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the user accessed the crime statistic */,
  "access_device" TEXT /* Device used to access the crime statistic */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_district_id FOREIGN KEY ("district_id") REFERENCES districts ("district_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_crime_id FOREIGN KEY ("crime_id") REFERENCES crimes ("crime_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "district_crime_rates" (
  "rate_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each crime rate */,
  "district_id" INTEGER /* ID of the district */,
  "crime_id" INTEGER /* ID of the crime type */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the crime rate was calculated */,
  "rate" REAL /* Crime rate (per 100,000 people) */,
  "crime_rate_rank" INTEGER /* Rank of the district's crime rate compared to other districts */,
  "crime_rate_trend" TEXT /* Trend of the district's crime rate (e.g., increasing, decreasing) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rate_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_district_crime_rates_crime_id FOREIGN KEY ("crime_id") REFERENCES crimes ("crime_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_district_crime_rates_district_id FOREIGN KEY ("district_id") REFERENCES districts ("district_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "district_population" (
  "population_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each population record */,
  "district_id" INTEGER /* ID of the district */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the population data was recorded */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the district */,
  "population_growth_rate" REAL /* Population growth rate of the district */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("population_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_district_population_district_id FOREIGN KEY ("district_id") REFERENCES districts ("district_id")
)
